changes.evidence: The initial ClientHello is included in the handshake transcript as a synthetic "message_hash" message, so only the hash value is needed for the handshake to complete, though the complete HelloRetryRequest contents are needed.

changes.evidence:

changes.evidence:
ServerHello

changes.change_action: must be assigned a specific sequence number
changes.evidence: In DTLS, each handshake message is assigned a specific sequence number.

changes.evidence:
Certificate
    (message_seq=1)

changes.related_state_or_step: example handshake; Record 1 and retransmitted Record 3 from server

changes.evidence:
Finished
                                                 (message_seq=4)

changes.change_condition: first message each side transmits in each association
changes.evidence: The first message each side transmits in each association always has message_seq = 0.

changes.change_condition: post-handshake message exchange in DTLS 1.3
changes.evidence: However, in DTLS 1.3 the message_seq is not reset, to allow distinguishing a retransmission from a previously sent post-handshake message from a newly sent post-handshake message.

changes.change_condition: when a handshake message is received and its message_seq matches next_receive_seq
changes.evidence: When a handshake message is received, if its message_seq value matches next_receive_seq, next_receive_seq is incremented and the message is processed.

changes.change_condition: when a handshake message is received and the sequence number is greater than next_receive_seq
changes.evidence: If the sequence number is greater than next_receive_seq, the implementation SHOULD queue the message but MAY discard it.

changes.change_condition: when a handshake message is received and the sequence number is less than next_receive_seq
changes.evidence: If the sequence number is less than next_receive_seq, the message MUST be discarded.

changes.change_condition: when a message is retransmitted
changes.change_action: reuse old value / must not increment
changes.evidence: When a message is retransmitted, the old message_seq value is reused, i.e., not incremented.

changes.change_condition: whenever a new message is generated
changes.evidence: Whenever a new message is generated, the message_seq value is incremented by one.

changes.change_condition: Handling a request considered excessive
changes.change_action: validated upper-bound exception / may return fewer than requested
changes.new_value: fewer than num_cids CIDs, including no CIDs at all
changes.related_state_or_step: Sending NewConnectionId in response to excessive RequestConnectionId
changes.evidence: An endpoint MAY handle requests which it considers excessive by responding with a NewConnectionId message containing fewer than num_cids CIDs, including no CIDs at all.

changes.change_condition: Responding to RequestConnectionId
changes.related_state_or_step: Sending NewConnectionId in response to RequestConnectionId
changes.evidence: Endpoints SHOULD respond to RequestConnectionId by sending a NewConnectionId with usage "cid_spare" containing num_cids CIDs as soon as possible.

changes.change_action: validated range check for encrypted_record demultiplexing
changes.evidence: 31 < OCT < 64 -+--> |DTLSCiphertext  |

changes.change_condition: OCT == 20
changes.change_action: must equal mapped constant for ChangeCipherSpec demultiplexing
changes.evidence: OCT == 20   -+--> ChangeCipherSpec (DTLS <1.3)

changes.change_condition: OCT == 21
changes.change_action: must equal mapped constant for Alert demultiplexing
changes.evidence: OCT == 21   -+--> Alert (Plaintext)

changes.change_condition: OCT == 22
changes.change_action: must equal mapped constant for DTLSHandshake demultiplexing
changes.evidence: OCT == 22   -+--> DTLSHandshake (Plaintext)

changes.change_condition: OCT == 23
changes.change_action: must equal mapped constant for Application Data demultiplexing
changes.evidence: OCT == 23   -+--> Application Data (DTLS <1.3)

changes.change_condition: OCT == 24
changes.change_action: must equal mapped constant for Heartbeat demultiplexing
changes.evidence: OCT == 24   -+--> Heartbeat (DTLS <1.3)

changes.change_condition: OCT == 25
changes.change_action: must equal mapped constant for encrypted_record with CID demultiplexing
changes.evidence: OCT == 25   -+--> DTLSCiphertext with CID (DTLS 1.2)

changes.change_condition: OCT == 26
changes.change_action: must equal mapped constant for ACK demultiplexing
changes.evidence: OCT == 26   -+--> ACK (DTLS 1.3, Plaintext)

changes.change_condition: else
changes.change_action: invalid if value check fails; reject packet
changes.evidence: else      | ... |  Reject |

changes.change_condition: in ACK message format
changes.evidence: RecordNumber record_numbers<0..2^16-1>;

changes.change_condition: in some cases it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers
changes.evidence: it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers.

changes.change_condition: when included in an ACK
changes.change_action: must be in numerically increasing order
changes.evidence: record_numbers:  A list of the records containing handshake messages in the current flight which the endpoint has received and either processed or buffered, in numerically increasing order.

changes.change_condition: if the S bit is 0
changes.evidence: This value is 16 bits if the S bit is set to 1, and 8 bits if the S bit is 0.

changes.change_condition: if the S bit is set to 1
changes.evidence: Sequence Number:  The low-order 8 or 16 bits of the record sequence number.  This value is 16 bits if the S bit is set to 1

changes.change_condition: when records are encrypted
changes.new_value: encrypted by XOR with a generated mask
changes.evidence: record sequence numbers are also encrypted.  The basic pattern is that the underlying encryption algorithm used with the AEAD algorithm is used to generate a mask which is then XORed with the sequence number.

changes.change_condition: when the S bit is 0
changes.evidence: S:  The S bit (0x08) indicates the size of the sequence number. 0 means an 8-bit sequence number

changes.change_condition: when the S bit is 1
changes.evidence: S:  The S bit (0x08) indicates the size of the sequence number. 0 means an 8-bit sequence number, 1 means 16-bit.

changes.change_condition: Any data is received after a valid received closure alert
changes.change_action: must be compared as part of an epoch/sequence number pair; data with a pair after that of the valid received closure alert must be ignored
changes.related_state_or_step: processing received data after closure alert
changes.evidence: Any data received with an epoch/sequence number pair after that of a valid received closure alert MUST be ignored.

changes.change_condition: For DTLSPlaintext serialization
changes.new_value: low order 48 bits of the 64 bit sequence number
changes.evidence: The sequence number is set to be the low order 48 bits of the 64 bit sequence number.

changes.change_condition: When sending plaintext records
changes.new_value: MUST NOT be sent with sequence numbers that would exceed 2^48-1
changes.evidence: Plaintext records MUST NOT be sent with sequence numbers that would exceed 2^48-1, so the upper 16 bits will always be 0.

changes.change_condition: after selecting the most recent past epoch with matching bits
changes.new_value: reconstruct the sequence number for that epoch as described above
changes.related_state_or_step: reconstructing the Sequence Number and Epoch
changes.evidence: and then reconstruct the sequence number for that epoch as described above.

changes.change_condition: before a received record has been deprotected successfully
changes.change_action: must not update validation window state based on this sequence number
changes.evidence: The window MUST NOT be updated due to a received record until that record has been deprotected successfully.

changes.change_condition: for AEAD computation in DTLS 1.3
changes.new_value: 64-bit sequence number used as the sequence number for the AEAD computation
changes.evidence: In DTLS 1.3 the 64-bit sequence_number is used as the sequence number for the AEAD computation; unlike DTLS 1.2, the epoch is not included.

changes.change_condition: for each epoch
changes.evidence: with each sequence_number initially being 0 for each epoch

changes.change_condition: for each received record in an epoch
changes.change_action: must not equal any other received sequence number in that epoch during the lifetime of the association
changes.evidence: For each received record, the receiver MUST verify that the record contains a sequence number that does not duplicate the sequence number of any other record received in that epoch during the lifetime of the association.

changes.change_condition: if a record contains a sequence number lower than the left edge of the receive window
changes.evidence: Records that contain sequence numbers lower than the "left" edge of the window are rejected.

changes.change_condition: if the epoch bits match those of the current epoch
changes.new_value: the full sequence number which is numerically closest to one plus the sequence number of the highest successfully deprotected record in the current epoch
changes.related_state_or_step: reconstructing the Sequence Number and Epoch
changes.evidence: implementations SHOULD reconstruct the sequence number by computing the full sequence number which is numerically closest to one plus the sequence number of the highest successfully deprotected record in the current epoch.

changes.change_condition: if the received record falls within the window
changes.change_action: must be checked against a list of received records within the window
changes.evidence: Records falling within the window are checked against a list of received records within the window.

changes.change_condition: if the received record falls within the window and is new, or if the record is to the right of the window
changes.evidence: If the received record falls within the window and is new, or if the record is to the right of the window, then the record is new.

changes.change_condition: prior to allowing the sequence number to wrap
changes.change_action: must not wrap; invalid if wrap would occur without abandoning association or rekeying
changes.evidence: Implementations MUST either abandon an association or rekey prior to allowing the sequence number to wrap.

changes.change_condition: when a message is retransmitted as a new record
changes.evidence: From the perspective of the DTLS record layer, the retransmission is a new record.  This record will have a new DTLSPlaintext.sequence_number value.

changes.change_condition: when an epoch is first used
changes.related_state_or_step: received record counter initialization for an epoch
changes.evidence: The received record counter for an epoch MUST be initialized to zero when that epoch is first used.

changes.change_condition: when applying sequence number encryption
changes.change_action: must be present / must be absent
changes.new_value: encryption applied only to DTLSCiphertext sequence number
changes.evidence: Note that sequence number encryption is only applied to the DTLSCiphertext structure and not to the DTLSPlaintext structure, even though it also contains a sequence number.

changes.change_condition: when computing the encrypted sequence number
changes.old_value: on-the-wire representation of the sequence number
changes.new_value: XOR of the on-the-wire representation of the sequence number with the leading bytes of the mask
changes.evidence: The encrypted sequence number is computed by XORing the leading bytes of the mask with the on-the-wire representation of the sequence number.

changes.change_condition: when decrypting the encrypted sequence number
changes.new_value: decrypted by the same XOR process
changes.evidence: Decryption is accomplished by the same process.

changes.change_condition: when expanded into RecordNumber
changes.new_value: combined with epoch into an unpacked RecordNumber structure
changes.evidence: When expanded, the epoch and sequence number can be combined into an unpacked RecordNumber structure

changes.change_condition: EncryptedExtensions is received prior to receiving a ServerHello
changes.change_action: must not treat EncryptedExtensions as safely acknowledgeable before ServerHello is received
changes.evidence: a client might receive an EncryptedExtensions message prior to receiving a ServerHello. Because it cannot decrypt the EncryptedExtensions, it cannot safely acknowledge it

changes.change_condition: record content type is other than handshake
changes.evidence: ACKs MUST NOT be sent for records of any content type other than handshake

changes.change_condition: If it is set to "cid_spare"
changes.evidence: If it is set to "cid_spare", then either an existing or new CID MAY be used.

changes.change_condition: If usage is set to "cid_immediate"
changes.evidence: If usage is set to "cid_immediate", then one of the new CIDs MUST be used immediately for all future records.

changes.change_condition: Responding to RequestConnectionId
changes.related_state_or_step: Sending NewConnectionId in response to RequestConnectionId
changes.evidence: Endpoints SHOULD respond to RequestConnectionId by sending a NewConnectionId with usage "cid_spare" containing num_cids CIDs as soon as possible.
